title: prunus × subhirtella var. koshiensis
intro: Prunus × subhirtella var. koshiensis, also known as Koshi White Pear or Koshi Cherry, is a deciduous tree or shrub that is a variety of flowering cherry native to East Asia. The plant has medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are derived from its leaves, flowers, and bark. The health benefits of Prunus × subhirtella var. koshiensis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its various parts are also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues.
